data.title: Stablecoin protocol Falcon Finance TVL exceeds $80 million
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to official data, the TVL of the stablecoin protocol Falcon Finance exceeds 80 million US dollars and is currently reported at 88.9 million US dollars. The APY of its pledged version token sUSDF is currently reported at 22.6%.
It is reported that Falcon Finance is a synthetic US dollar stablecoin protocol launched by DWF Labs partner Andrei Grachev.

data.title: CZ replied and mentioned LUXUN
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News CZ replied on the X platform: "Infinite distance, countless people, are all related to BSC. -Lu Xun" Affected by this, LUXUN rose to a new high.
According to GMGN market data, LUXUN's market value rose to 9m in a short period of time.

data.title: Ripple USD Market Cap Reaches Record High
data.description: According to PANews, the market capitalization of Ripple's stablecoin, Ripple USD (RLUSD), has surpassed $170 million, reaching an all-time high of $176,801,211, as reported by Coingecko. This milestone follows Ripple's recent announcement that the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has officially withdrawn its four-year-long lawsuit against the company.

data.title: Musk's Department Blocks $52 Million Payment to World Economic Forum
data.description: According to PANews, the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by Elon Musk, has halted a $52 million payment to the World Economic Forum (WEF). Musk confirmed the action on his social media platform, X, responding to a post about the news with the comment, "It's true, you're welcome."

data.title: a16z Urges SEC for Token Issuance Guidance to Retain Blockchain Ownership
data.description: According to Odaily, venture capital firm a16z has called on the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to provide clear guidance on token issuance. During a meeting with the SEC's cryptocurrency task force, led by Commissioner Hester Peirce, a16z emphasized that such guidance could prevent the trend of issuing tokens exclusively to non-U.S. citizens. This trend, they argue, effectively transfers the ownership of U.S.-developed blockchain technology overseas.

data.title: a16z: If the SEC provides guidance on token issuance, it can prevent the transfer of ownership of blockchain technology developed in the United States to overseas
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News a16z said at the cryptocurrency working group meeting of Hester Peirce, Commissioner of the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission: "If the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission can provide guidance on token issuance, it will prevent the trend of token issuance only to non-U.S. citizens, which is actually transferring the ownership of blockchain technology developed in the United States overseas."
